metadata.source: Harris (1996, 2010 ed.) globulars; curated nebulae/black holes/clusters from SIMBAD and standard references

objects.name: Sagittarius A*
objects.desc: Supermassive black hole at the Galactic Centre — 4.15 million M☉

objects.name: Cygnus X-1
objects.desc: Stellar-mass black hole (~21 M☉) accreting from a blue supergiant; first widely accepted black-hole candidate

objects.name: GRO J1655-40
objects.desc: Microquasar — stellar-mass black hole (~6 M☉) with relativistic jets

objects.name: GRS 1915+105
objects.desc: Microquasar — rapidly spinning stellar-mass black hole (~12 M☉)

objects.name: A0620-00
objects.desc: Nearest known black hole X-ray binary (~6.6 M☉)

objects.name: V404 Cygni
objects.desc: Black-hole X-ray binary (~9 M☉) known for dramatic outbursts

objects.name: Orion Nebula (M42)
objects.desc: Nearest large star-forming region — emission nebula in Orion's sword

objects.name: Lagoon Nebula (M8)
objects.desc: Giant emission nebula and active star-forming region in Sagittarius

objects.name: Trifid Nebula (M20)
objects.desc: Combined emission, reflection and dark nebula in Sagittarius

objects.name: Eagle Nebula (M16)
objects.desc: Star-forming region hosting the 'Pillars of Creation'

objects.name: North America Nebula
objects.desc: Large emission nebula in Cygnus shaped like the continent

objects.name: Heart Nebula (IC 1805)
objects.desc: Emission nebula and open cluster in Cassiopeia

objects.name: Veil / Cygnus Loop
objects.desc: Supernova remnant — expanding shell from a star that exploded ~10–20 kyr ago

objects.name: Crab Nebula (M1)
objects.desc: Supernova remnant from SN 1054, powered by the Crab pulsar

objects.name: SN 1006 remnant
objects.desc: Remnant of the brightest stellar event in recorded history (SN 1006)

objects.name: Cassiopeia A
objects.desc: Youngest known galactic supernova remnant (~350 yr); strong radio source

objects.name: Omega Nebula (M17)
objects.desc: Bright star-forming emission nebula in Sagittarius

objects.name: Carina Nebula
objects.desc: Vast star-forming region hosting Eta Carinae and Trumpler clusters

objects.name: Eta Carinae
objects.desc: Luminous blue variable (~5 million L☉) — candidate future supernova

objects.name: Taurus Molecular Cloud
objects.desc: Nearby cold molecular cloud — low-mass star-forming region

objects.name: Rho Ophiuchi cloud
objects.desc: Nearest active star-forming molecular cloud complex

objects.name: Vela Molecular Ridge
objects.desc: Massive molecular cloud complex along the Vela arm

objects.name: Pleiades (M45)
objects.desc: Young open cluster of hot blue stars (~100 Myr) in Taurus

objects.name: Hyades
objects.desc: Nearest open cluster to the Sun (~625 Myr)

objects.name: Double Cluster (h+χ Per)
objects.desc: Pair of young open clusters in Perseus

objects.name: IC 2602 (Southern Pleiades)
objects.desc: Bright young open cluster in Carina

objects.name: Large Magellanic Cloud
objects.desc: Satellite dwarf galaxy of the Milky Way (~50 kpc); off the disk

objects.name: NGC 104 (47 Tuc)
objects.desc: Globular cluster — ancient gravitationally bound swarm of 10⁴–10⁶ stars in the galactic halo
